_meta.description: Systematic exploration of the entire YeshID site. Visits every known page, collects the a11y snapshot (all interactive elements, labels, roles), and returns a structured site map. Output is used to update site.model.json with verified selectors and to give an LLM enough context to act on any instruction.
_meta.note: Run this payload whenever the YeshID UI may have changed, or when an LLM needs to act on an instruction it has never seen before.

chain.code:
// Iterate all known pages and collect snapshots. In production runtime this would be a loop action; here it's a multi-navigate chain.
(async () => { const results = {}; const pages = {{pages}}; for (const page of pages) { try { window.location.href = '{{base_url}}' + page.url; await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1200)); // Run exploration script per page const pageData = await ({{explorationScript}}); results[page.name] = pageData; } catch(e) { results[page.name] = { error: e.message }; } } return results; })()
chain.note: Full site exploration — collects interactive element snapshots from all known pages.

chain.code:
// Return summary statistics
(function() { const map = window.__yeshie_explore_results__ || {}; return { pagesExplored: Object.keys(map).length, summary: Object.entries(map).map(([name, data]) => ({ page: name, inputCount: data.inputs?.length || 0, buttonCount: data.buttons?.length || 0, tableCount: data.tables?.length || 0, affordancesFound: data.affordances?.length || 0 })) }; })()

outputFormat.description: The site_map result from s2 should be written to sites/yeshid/exploration-results.json. Each page entry contains: navLinks, headings, inputs (with vuetifyLabel), buttons, tables (with headers and sample rows), affordances (icon buttons with discovered tooltips).
useFor: Populating site.model.json abstractTargets with verified cachedSelectors
Answering ad-hoc LLM queries about what's on a given page
Detecting UI changes between exploration runs (diff exploration-results.json versions)
Building CoWork's prompt context for free-form instructions like 'add the GitHub integration'
